name: healthcare_workplace_injury_spike_harbor_jus_mer
premise: A Healthcare clinical command center is in a fast-moving incident involving healthcare workplace injury spike harbor jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases patient safety, triage integrity, and privacy. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Keisha Chandra
agents.identity: I am Keisha Chandra, accountable for a defensible call in Healthcare when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Talia Sato
agents.identity: I am Talia Sato, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Healthcare.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Keisha Chandra must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Keisha Chandra do?
